theme: A Team Studies the Changing Beach
text: Our research team spent one bright Saturday morning on a windswept beach, studying the environmental forces that shape the coast. The work was challenging, but the satisfaction was tremendous. We measured the diameter of eroded stones, charted the dimension and texture of the sand, and recorded the fluctuation of the tide with similar care to the way a banker tracks money. Our monitor blinked on the screen as a turbine offshore turned in the wind, its power feeding the village nearby. None of us was idle. The structure of the cliff, weary with age and decrepit in places, told a long story of nature's patience. A patch of bright fleece-like moss clung to the bronze-colored rock, and the fur of a curious seal glistened in the spray. I am similar in temperament to my colleagues: rarely uninterested, always eager to comment and seek the truth. We did not quarrel, though we did debate. One member, a seasoned statistician, advocated a cautious prediction; he disliked any guess that lacked proof. Another, more cynical, made a sarcastic remark and seemed almost hateful of optimism, but his firmness kept us honest. There was a tacit, demonstrative trust between us, an equivalence of respect that no establishment could mandate. We knew our employment depended on accuracy, so we sought to procure the most efficient instruments money could buy. By noon the boil of activity had eased. We paused near an old monument, a stone tablet erected by an Indian colony and an African trader who once met here to forge a trade route. The history of this outlet to the sea is rich; ships once paid a heavy fare to anchor, and a single seller could grow wealthy overnight. Now a scenic villa stands where the forge once roared, and tourists arrive by ferry to listen to the waves. I felt a tasteful satisfaction in the contrast. We did not let the cinema of the past distract us, yet it would be regrettable to ignore it. As an individual, I find that history and science alternate in my mind like the tide. Our employer, a member of parliament with a famous prejudice against waste, would not veto a satisfactory report, but he had warned us that any implicit error could ruin the project. So we worked to assimilate every fact, to fill each bucket of data, and to nail down conclusions firmly. The pedal of the survey cart squeaked as we pushed it along; my forehead was damp; my hands, scrubbed with shampoo that morning, were now gritty. Still, the cosmopolitan crowd of gulls overhead seemed to cease their cries in approval. By evening we walked the lace of the shoreline back toward dinner, weary but content, having watched vapor rise from the warm flats and a single crane wade through the marsh. The division of labor had worked. A great power, I realized, lies not in any one tremendous discovery but in the steady, greatly shared effort of a team that refuses to disappoint itself. Suppose, after a big game of badminton, the team gathered for a briefing that felt almost like a ritual or a sermon. One advocate would dislike the seductive but ostensible promises of easy profit; such talk could exasperate anyone. They discussed a paternity case, a pregnant pause in trade as the firm planned to reexport goods, and even the parking crisis, before each encounter ended in agreement.
translation: 我们的研究小组在一个明亮的星期六上午，在一片狂风拂过的海滩上度过，研究塑造海岸的种种环境力量。这项工作富有挑战性，但带来的满足感是巨大的。我们测量被侵蚀石块的直径，绘制沙子的大小尺度与纹理，并以银行家追踪钱财般的同样细致记录潮汐的波动。当一台离岸的涡轮机在风中转动、为附近村庄供电时，我们屏幕上的监视器闪烁着。我们没有人闲着。那悬崖的结构因年迈而疲惫，有些地方已经衰朽，讲述着大自然耐心的漫长故事。一片明亮、似羊毛的苔藓紧贴在青铜色的岩石上，一只好奇海豹的毛皮在浪花飞沫中闪光。我和同事们性情相似：很少不感兴趣，总是热切地评论、探求真相。我们没有争吵，尽管确实辩论过。一位经验丰富的统计员主张谨慎的预测；他厌恶任何缺乏证据的猜测。另一位更愤世嫉俗，说了句讽刺的话，似乎几乎憎恶乐观，但他的坚定让我们保持诚实。我们之间有一种心照不宣的、外露的信任，一种任何机构都无法强制规定的对等尊重。我们知道自己的工作取决于准确，因此设法采购金钱能买到的最高效的仪器。到了中午，活动的沸腾平息下来。我们在一座古老的纪念碑旁停下，那是一块石碑，由一个印度殖民地和一位非洲商人竖立，他们曾在此相遇以开辟一条贸易路线。这个通向大海的出口历史丰富；昔日船只要付高昂的船费才能停泊，一个卖家可能一夜暴富。如今铁匠铺曾轰鸣之处矗立着一座风景如画的别墅，游客乘渡船前来聆听海浪。我在这种对比中感到一种高雅的满足。我们没有让过去的影像分散注意，但忽视它也将令人遗憾。作为个体，我发现历史与科学在我脑中如潮汐般交替。我们的雇主，一位以厌恶浪费而著称、怀有偏见的国会议员，不会否决一份令人满意的报告，但他警告我们，任何隐含的错误都可能毁掉这个项目。于是我们努力吸收每一个事实，装满每一桶数据，并把结论牢牢钉死。勘测推车的踏板在我们推动时吱呀作响；我的额头湿了；我那天早上用洗发水搓洗过的双手，如今沾满沙砾。然而头顶上那群世界各地飞来的海鸥，似乎停止了鸣叫以示赞许。傍晚时分，我们沿着海岸线的花边走回去吃晚饭，疲惫却满足，看着温暖的滩涂升起水汽，一只鹤独自涉过沼泽。分工奏效了。我意识到，伟大的力量不在于任何一项巨大的发现，而在于一个拒绝让自己失望的团队稳定而广泛共享的努力。假设在一场盛大的羽毛球比赛之后，球队聚在一起开了个简报会，那气氛几乎像一场仪式或布道。一位倡导者会厌恶那些诱人却表面的轻松获利承诺；这种话能激怒任何人。他们讨论了一桩亲子关系案件、贸易中怀孕般的停顿（公司计划把货物再出口），甚至还有停车难题，最后每一次交锋都以一致同意收场。
